title: What does cashflow at your dream level feel like?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for building the felt sense of massive cashflow before it arrives.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for examining the current ceiling on income expectations and imagining what it would actually feel like to operate at a much higher level.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when a large income figure feels abstract or unlikely rather than something you can inhabit.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. The prompts move from naming the current ceiling to inhabiting a much larger figure.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What monthly income figure currently feels laughable rather than possible?
Write the number. Then write one sentence on where that reaction comes from.
What is the story I have told myself about who gets to earn at that level?
Write it out plainly. Who is that story about, and where did it come from?
If that figure arrived next month, what would a normal Tuesday morning look like?
Write the scene in plain detail: where you are, what you are doing, what the account balance reads.
What would I need to believe about myself for that level of cashflow to feel ordinary?
Write three beliefs. Start each with: "I am someone who..."
What is one thing I can do today that signals I expect this level of cashflow?
Write the specific action. It does not have to be large.
Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
